model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Georgia holds a meaningful edge in overall efficiency, ranking 31st in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of +20.47 compared to Saint Louis's +18.32 at 41st, driven primarily by a significantly better offense (AdjO 124.7, 15th nationally vs 119.5, 51st) that more than offsets Saint Louis's defensive advantage (AdjD 101.2 vs 104.2). The Bulldogs' 10-9 SEC record reflects battle-testing against far superior competition than Saint Louis's A-10 slate, and the Billikens enter in concerning form having lost three of their last six games—including a 29-point blowout to George Mason—before falling to Dayton in the A-10 Tournament semifinal. With the spread at Georgia -1.5 and the Bulldogs' elite offensive firepower projected to exploit Saint Louis's good-but-not-great defense, Georgia's SEC-caliber talent and offensive ceiling give them the narrow edge in this classic 8/9 coin-flip.

reasoningStep.title: Midwest 8/9: Georgia vs Saint Louis
reasoningStep.summary: Georgia's elite offense (15th nationally) and SEC schedule strength give them a narrow edge over a Saint Louis team that stumbled badly down the stretch, losing 3 of their last 6 including a 29-point blowout. Georgia's ~5-point offensive efficiency advantage per 100 possessions exceeds Saint Louis's ~3-point defensive edge, and the 1.5-point spread confirms Georgia as a slight favorite.
evidence: KenPom: Georgia #31 (AdjEM +20.47, AdjO 124.7/15th, AdjD 104.2) vs Saint Louis #41 (AdjEM +18.32, AdjO 119.5/51st, AdjD 101.2)
Torvik: Georgia #37 (AdjOE 124.3/18th, AdjDE 104.7/93rd) vs Saint Louis #44 (AdjOE 119.5/45th, AdjDE 102.2/56th)
Georgia 22-10 (10-9 SEC); Saint Louis 28-5 (16-4 A-10) but lost 3 of last 6 games including 29-point loss to George Mason
Saint Louis lost to Dayton in A-10 Tournament semifinal; Georgia lost to Ole Miss 76-72 in SEC Tournament

The Bulldogs' record in SEC games is 10-9, and their record is 12-1 in non-conference play. Georgia ranks seventh in the SEC with 23.9 defensive rebounds per game led by Kanon Catchings averaging 3.5. The Billikens' record in A-10 games is 16-4. Saint Louis averages 18.3 assists per game to lead the A-10, paced by Robbie Avila with 4.1. Georgia makes 47.1% of its shots from the field this season, which is 9.2 percentage points higher than Saint Louis has allowed to its opponents (37.9%). Saint Louis averages 8.0 more points per game (87.2) than Georgia gives up (79.2). TOP PERFORMERS: Jeremiah Wilkinson is scoring 17.0 points per game and averaging 2.0 rebounds for the Bulldogs. Catchings is averaging 3.3 made 3-pointers over the last 10 games. Avila is averaging 12.9 points and 4.1 assists for the Billikens. Dion Brown is averaging 11.6 points over the last 10 games. LAST 10 GAMES: Bulldogs: 6-4, averaging 84.3 points, 29.4 rebounds, 15.0 assists, 7.4 steals and 3.8 blocks per game while shooting 47.7% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 82.5 points per game. Billikens: 6-4, averaging 77.8 points, 31.4 rebounds, 16.1 assists, 6.4 steals and 4.2 blocks per game while shooting 47.9% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 72.8 points. ------ The Associated Press created this story using technology provided by Data Skrive and data from Sportradar . The Bulldogs' record in SEC games is 10-9, and their record is 12-1 in non-conference play. Georgia ranks seventh in the SEC with 23.9 defensive rebounds per game led by Kanon Catchings averaging 3.5. The Billikens' record in A-10 games is 16-4. Saint Louis averages 18.3 assists per game to lead the A-10, paced by Robbie Avila with 4.1. Georgia makes 47.1% of its shots from the field this season, which is 9.2 percentage points higher than Saint Louis has allowed to its opponents (37.9%). Saint Louis averages 8.0 more points per game (87.2) than Georgia gives up (79.2). TOP PERFORMERS: Jeremiah Wilkinson is scoring 17.0 points per game and averaging 2.0 rebounds for the Bulldogs. Catchings is averaging 3.3 made 3-pointers over the last 10 games. Avila is averaging 12.9 points and 4.1 assists for the Billikens. Dion Brown is averaging 11.6 points over the last 10 games. LAST 10 GAMES: Bulldogs: 6-4, averaging 84.3 points, 29.4 rebounds, 15.0 assists, 7.4 steals and 3.8 blocks per game while shooting 47.7% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 82.5 points per game. Billikens: 6-4, averaging 77.8 points, 31.4 rebounds, 16.1 assists, 6.4 steals and 4.2 blocks per game while shooting 47.9% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 72.8 points. ------ The Associated Press created this story using technology provided by Data Skrive and data from Sportradar . TV: CBS (Brian Anderson, play-by-play; Jim Jackson, analyst; Allie LaForce, sideline reporter) Video Streaming: NCAA.com National Radio: Westwood One (Jason Benetti, play-by-play; Sarah Kustok, analyst) Georgia Radio: Georgia Bulldog Network (Scott Howard, play-by-play; Chuck Dowdle, analyst; Adam Gillespie, producer) Audio Streaming: WestwoodOneSports.com/madness The Starting 5 The winningest and highest-scoring Georgia Bulldogs ever will open play in the 2026 NCAA Tournament on Thursday when they face Saint Louis in the Round of 64 at 9:45 p.m. ET in Buffalo, N.Y. Georgia's 22 regular-season victories are the most ever by the Bulldogs in 121 seasons of competition and is two shy of UGA's most overall wins in a season – 24 in 1982-83 and 1996-97. The Dogs have scored 2,874 points in 32 games, passing UGA's previous mark of 2,764 points in 37 outings in 2023-24. Georgia is averaging 89.8 ppg, 7.1 ppg over its current record – 82.7 ppg in 1989-90. Georgia has earned a second straight NCAA bid for just the fourth time in program history. Now I have a good picture. Let me compile the key data:

**Georgia (8-seed, SEC)**
- KenPom: #31, AdjEM +20.47, AdjO 124.7 (15th), AdjD 104.2
- Torvik: #37, AdjOE 124.3 (18th), AdjDE 104.7 (93rd)
- Record: 22-10 (10-9 SEC)
- SEC 7-seed, lost to Ole Miss 76-72 in SEC Tournament
- Described as "winningest and highest-scoring Georgia Bulldogs ever"
- Spread: Georgia -1.5, O/U 169.5

**Saint Louis (9-seed, A-10)**
- KenPom: #41, AdjEM +18.32, AdjO 119.5 (51st), AdjD 101.2
- Torvik: #44, AdjOE 119.5 (45th), AdjDE 102.2 (56th)
- Record: 28-5 (16-4 A-10)
- Won A-10 regular season title but lost to Dayton in A-10 Tournament semifinal
- Lost 3 of last 6 games entering tournament (including 29-point loss to George Mason)
- ESPN noted: "went 3-3 in its six games entering the conference tournament, losing those three games by a combined 49 points"

Key observations:
- Georgia has a clear offensive advantage: AdjO 124.7 vs 119.5 (~5 points per 100 possessions)
- Saint Louis has a better defense: AdjD 101.2 vs 104.2 (~3 points per 100 possessions)
- Net: Georgia's efficiency margin is ~2 points better
- Georgia's SEC schedule is significantly tougher than Saint Louis's A-10 schedule
- Saint Louis is in poor recent form (3-3 in last 6, including a 29-point blowout loss)
- Georgia favored by 1.5 points
- Georgia's offense is elite (15th nationally) while Saint Louis's defense is solid but not elite enough to neutralize it
